---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```

```{css}

body{
  font-family: "Segoe UI", "Helvetica", "Arial", sans-serif;
  background-color: #fffde6;
  color: black;
  line-height: 1.6;
  margin: 20px;
}

h1{
  font-size: 32px;
  color: black;
}

h2{
  font-size: 24px;
  color: black;
}

h3{
  font-size: 20px;
  color: black;
}

p{
  font-size: 16px;
}

img{
  margin-top: 15px;
  margin-bottom: 15px;
}

.dynamic-text{
  color: black;
  font-weight: bold;
}

```

## Observing Phone Use Among Bus Passengers

```{r data_prep, error=FALSE}

# Load necessary libraries for data manipulation and visualization
suppressPackageStartupMessages(try(library(tidyverse), silent = TRUE)) # Load the tidyverse package

# Read the CSV data from the provided Google Sheets link
logged_data <- read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vTeLDMHtbbSEKTXmlMHI_Xx56G79uUAATt1yyA4Q4eS5xRcV92sRXacFiX0nF8PNBGX_dTS03ihS4Kv/pub?gid=1547770&single=true&output=csv")

# Rename columns to simpler, R-friendly names for easier reference
latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(
    Date = `Date of observation`,
    Time = `Time of observation`,
    Phone_Users = `How many passengers are using their phones?`,
    Total_Passengers = `How many passengers can you see on the bus?`,
    Unusual_Observations = `Any unusual observations or notes?`,
    Main_activity = `What is the main activity most passangers are doing?`
  ) %>%
  mutate(Phone_Percent = ifelse(
    Total_Passengers > 0, 
    (Phone_Users / Total_Passengers) * 100,
    NA
  ))

latest_data$DateTime <- paste(latest_data$Date, latest_data$Time)
latest_data$DateTime <- factor(latest_data$DateTime, levels = unique(latest_data$DateTime))


```

# Summary statistics

```{r summary, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}

# Summary statistics for amount of people using phones
avg_users <- mean(latest_data$Phone_Users, na.rm = TRUE) # Average number of passengers using phones

comment1 <- paste0("On average, about ", round(avg_users), " passengers on the bus were using their phones.")
cat(comment1, "\n")

max_users <- max(latest_data$Phone_Users, na.rm = TRUE) # Maximum number of passengers using phones

comment2 <- paste0("The maximum number of passengers observed using phones on the bus was ", max_users, ".")
cat(comment2, "\n")

# Calculate summary statistics percentage of people using phones
avg_percent <- mean(latest_data$Phone_Percent, na.rm = TRUE) # Average phone usage percent

comment3 <- paste0("On average, about ", round(avg_percent, 1), "% of passengers on the bus were using their phones.")
cat(comment3, "\n")

max_percent <- max(latest_data$Phone_Percent, na.rm = TRUE) # Maximum phone usage percent

comment4 <- paste0("The maximum percentage of passengers observed using phones on the bus was ", round(max_percent, 1), "%.")
cat(comment4, "\n")

```

# Graphs

```{r plot1, echo=FALSE}

# Bar Chart 1: Percentage of passengers using phones over time
# Shows proportion of passengers using phones relative to total passengers observed at each observation time and date
# Tried using ratio, but using percentage was clearer for interpretation

p1 <- latest_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = DateTime, y = Phone_Percent)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") + 
  labs(title = "Percentage of Passengers Using Phones by Time and Date",
       x = "Time of Observation",
       y = "Percentage of passengers using phones (%)",
       caption = "Shows the percentage of passengers using phones at each observation time.") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) 

print(p1)

```

```{r plot2, echo=FALSE}

#Bar Chart 2: Main activities of passengers
p2 <- latest_data %>% 
  ggplot() +
  geom_bar(aes( x= Main_activity, fill = Main_activity)) +
  labs(title = "Main activities of passengers on the bus",
       x = "Time of Observation",
       y = "Main activities",
       caption = "Shows the range of activities passengers were engaged in during bus journeys, with phone use appearing as the
       most common behavior compared to other activities.")  +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axos.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

print(p2)

```


```{r, echo=FALSE, results='hide'}

# Loads the magick package
library(magick)
library(magrittr)

# --- Set the parameters for the meme panels ---

panel_width <- 320 # Width in pixels for each image/text panel.
panel_height <- 220 # Height in pixels for each image/text panel.

#Reading and resize the first image
first_image <- image_read("https://img.freepik.com/premium-photo/headphones-journal-woman-bus-travel-with-diary-planning-schedule-notes-university-writing-tech-person-public-transport-listening-music-with-notebook-college_590464-528053.jpg") %>%
               image_resize(paste0(panel_width, "x", panel_height, "!"))

#Reading and resize the second image
second_image <- image_read("https://media.istockphoto.com/id/1411636518/photo/multiracial-friends-talking-and-using-a-smartphone-while-riding-a-bus-in-the-city.jpg?s=612x612&w=0&k=20&c=zAm51DNPaD8OecC1Y81bp-u_PEXWRA_fHvXR5ebrIwM=") %>%
               image_resize(paste0(panel_width, "x", panel_height, "!"))

#Create first text panel
top_right_text <- image_blank(width = 600,
                              height = 180,
                              color = "#FFFFFF") %>%
  
  #Adding text to the blank panel
  image_annotate(text = "Me collecting data on bus passengers",
                 color = "#000000",
                 size = 35,
                 font = "Arial",
                 gravity = "center")

#Create second text panel
bottom_right_text <- image_blank(width = 600,
                                 height = 180,
                                 color = "#FFFFFF") %>%
  
  #Adding text to the blank panel
  image_annotate(text = "Everyone on their phones",
                 color = "#000000",
                 size = 50,
                 font = "Arial",
                 gravity = "center")

#Combines first row (top row) horizontally
first_row <- c(first_image, top_right_text) %>%
  image_append()

#Combines second row (bottom row) horizontally
second_row <- c(second_image, bottom_right_text) %>%
  image_append()

#Stacking the two rows vertically to make the full meme
meme <- c(first_row, second_row) %>%
  image_append(stack = TRUE)

#Opens drawing context to add lines
meme <- image_draw(meme)

#Drawing vertical line to split left and right panels
abline(v = panel_width, col="#000000", lwd = 5)

#Drawing horizontal line to split the top and bottom panels
abline(h = panel_height, col="#000000", lwd = 5)

#Close drawing context
dev.off()

#Saves the meme as a PNG file called "bus_meme.png" into folder
image_write(meme, "bus_meme.png")

```

# Bus Meme

![](bus_meme.png)
